summary: Acromegaly is a condition caused by too much growth hormone produced after bones stop growing during adulthood; thus, this hormonal disorder never manifests when children or adolescents have their cartilaginous ends at risk for further elongation through ossification processes typical up until skeletal maturity has been achieved via complete fusion of these regions into solid bony structures characteristic only post-adolescence/early twenties depending upon individual physiology but generally around age twenty-five years old give-or-take several decades based largely off genetic factors influencing timing of puberty onset among other influences like nutrition status etc.. The disease presents with enlarged hands/fingers/toes feet face jawline nose ears lips tongue along with thickened skin soft tissues muscles tendons ligaments joints all indicative signs pointing towards chronic overproduction HGH beyond normal levels necessary throughout life span once full physical development concludes sometime between late teens early midtwenties onward barring any medical intervention halting progression symptoms worsening steadily thereafter without treatment unless managed effectively reducing severity impact quality longevity patient’s overall healthspan lifespan potentially extending many more healthy active productive years free debilitating effects associated untreated severe cases where complications arise significantly compromising functional independence mobility cognitive abilities emotional well-being social relationships daily activities self-care routines long-term care needs requiring extensive support systems including family friends caregivers healthcare professionals community resources available local state national international organizations dedicated addressing rare endocrine disorders such as acromegaly providing comprehensive multidisciplinary approach ensuring best possible outcomes maximizing potential benefits improving patients’ lives enhancing dignity autonomy control over own destiny despite challenges faced living
